title: Silky Japanese Gelatin Jelly
description: A soothing, cultured Okinawan-inspired dish to comfort a sick day, using unflavored gelatin and a shio-koji cure that is vegan-friendly and silky smooth.
instructions: Combine shio-koji and 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to a simmer over low heat.
Remove from heat and let steep for 30 minutes.
Combine the soaked shio-koji mixture with the remaining 2 cups of water, gelatin, agar agar powder, kombu seaweed, katsuo dashi powder, and salt in a blender or food processor and blend until smooth.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl.
Add sugar if using and stir until dissolved.
Pour the mixture into a 9x13 inch baking dish or a silicone jelly mold and refrigerate until set, about 4 hours.
Unmold the jelly and cut into small cubes or slices before serving.
